summary: Southern Abrasives moved to won in Linear, creating an operational handoff and a possible marketing proof-point opportunity, but public naming and external announcement permission remain unresolved.

situations.label: Deal moved to won
situations.rationale: Linear records the deal moving from proposal to won, but downstream delivery, relationship sensitivity, and external announcement approval still need review.

goals: Prepare operational handoff without assuming public announcement permission.
Surface marketing opportunity for Maya without exposing unnecessary operational detail.
blockers: Public naming permission is not recorded.
Delivery handoff details are not linked.
open_questions: Can Southern Abrasives be named publicly?
Which delivery or onboarding follow-up is required?
What proof point, if any, is safe to use in external marketing?

next_actions.summary: Alex should confirm operational handoff, owner, next action, and any relationship-sensitive constraints.
Maya should review whether the won deal supports an internal proof-point note, customer story draft, LinkedIn draft, or no-op.
